annotations.label: Theory of relativity

anchor.snippet: comprises two physics theories by Albert Einstein

annotations.note: A physics theory, not a mathematical object formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Domain of special relativity

anchor.snippet: applies to all physical phenomena in the absence of gravity

annotations.note: A physical-scope claim with no mathematical statement to formalize in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General relativity and gravitation

anchor.snippet: explains the law of gravitation and its relation to the forces of nature

annotations.note: A physics claim about gravitation; Mathlib has no gravitation or field-theory content.

annotations.label: Special relativity

anchor.snippet: is a theory of the structure of spacetime

annotations.note: No spacetime or relativity structures exist in Mathlib (grep for spacetime/relativity returns nothing).

annotations.label: Two postulates of special relativity

anchor.snippet: is based on two postulates which are contradictory in classical mechanics

annotations.note: Physical postulates with no corresponding formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principle of relativity

anchor.snippet: The laws of physics are the same for all observers in any inertial frame of reference

annotations.note: A physical invariance principle, not a formalized mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Constancy of the speed of light

anchor.snippet: The speed of light in vacuum is the same for all observers, regardless of their relative motion

annotations.note: An empirical physical postulate with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Relativity of simultaneity

anchor.snippet: Two events, simultaneous for one observer, may not be simultaneous for another observer

annotations.note: A physics consequence of Lorentz transformations, which are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Time dilation

anchor.snippet: Moving clocks are measured to tick more slowly than an observer's

annotations.note: A relativistic physical effect with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Length contraction

anchor.snippet: Objects are measured to be shortened in the direction that they are moving

annotations.note: A relativistic physical effect with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Maximum speed is finite

anchor.snippet: No physical object, message or field line can travel faster than the speed of light

annotations.note: A physical speed-limit claim with no mathematical formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mass–energy equivalence

anchor.snippet: energy and mass are equivalent and transmutable

annotations.note: The physical relation E=mc² is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz transformations replace Galilean transformations

anchor.snippet: the replacement of the Galilean transformations of classical mechanics by the Lorentz transformations

annotations.note: Grep finds no Lorentz or Galilean transformation content anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General relativity

anchor.snippet: is a theory of gravitation developed by Einstein in the years

annotations.note: A physics theory of gravitation with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Equivalence principle

anchor.snippet: the states of accelerated motion and being at rest in a gravitational field

annotations.note: A physical principle with no corresponding formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free fall is inertial motion

anchor.snippet: an object in free fall is falling because that is how objects move when there is no force

annotations.note: A geodesic-motion physical claim not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Einstein field equations

anchor.snippet: he devised the Einstein field equations which relate the curvature of spacetime with the mass

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Einstein field equations or spacetime-curvature definitions.

annotations.label: Gravitational time dilation

anchor.snippet: Clocks run slower in deeper gravitational wells

annotations.note: A relativistic physical effect absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Precession of orbits

anchor.snippet: Orbits precess in a way unexpected in Newton's theory of gravity

annotations.note: A physical prediction of general relativity with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Light deflection

anchor.snippet: Rays of light bend in the presence of a gravitational field

annotations.note: A physical prediction of general relativity not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frame-dragging

anchor.snippet: Rotating masses "drag along" the spacetime around them

annotations.note: A relativistic physical effect with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Expansion of the universe

anchor.snippet: The universe is expanding, and certain components within the universe can accelerate

annotations.note: A cosmological physical claim with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Solutions of the field equations are metric tensors

anchor.snippet: The solutions of the field equations are metric tensors which define the topology of the spacetime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no field equations or Lorentzian metric-tensor/spacetime formalization.

annotations.label: Principle-theories

anchor.snippet: theories that start not from speculative constructs or imagined mechanisms

annotations.note: A philosophy-of-physics classification, not a mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Relativity is falsifiable

anchor.snippet: It makes predictions that can be tested by experiment

annotations.note: An epistemological claim about empirical testability, not formalizable in Mathlib.
